title: Introduction to Web & HTML/CSS
sub: Client-server · HTTP · HTML5 · CSS · Bootstrap · Box model

h: Client-server architecture and HTTP request-response cycle
HTML5 semantic elements — header, footer, section, article, nav
CSS box model — margin, padding, border, content (diagram)
CSS selectors — element, class, id, attribute, universal
Responsive web design with media queries
Block vs inline elements
m: Bootstrap grid system and responsive layout
Forms and input types in HTML
Tables, lists, multimedia tags in HTML
Rowspan and colspan in HTML tables
l: DNS lookup flow and browser rendering basics

qs.t: What is the CSS box model? Explain margin, padding, border, and content with a neat diagram. Differentiate between block and inline elements with examples.
Define CSS selectors. Explain different types of CSS selectors (element, class, id, attribute, universal) with examples.
What are CSS combinators? Explain different types with suitable examples.
Define media queries. Explain responsive web design using media queries with examples.
What is Bootstrap? Explain the role of Bootstrap in responsive web design.
Explain the rowspan and colspan attributes in HTML tables with examples. What are the attributes of list tags? Explain with suitable examples.
Describe the structure of an HTML document and explain different types of CSS. Write an HTML code snippet to demonstrate the basic structure with sectioning elements.
What is the Internet and the Web? Explain the importance of Web technology and describe the concept of Web 2.0 with its key features.
Describe the concept of the client/server computing paradigm and its role in modern computing. Illustrate with real-world examples of applications that utilize this architecture.
Explain the concept of multi-tier architecture in web development. Illustrate with a diagram a three-tier architecture consisting of: Presentation layer, Business logic layer, and Data layer.

Create an HTML page containing an iframe within a paragraph (source: http://www.tuiost.edu.np, dimensions: 200px height, 400px width). Include tags for header, article, and footer. Insert a link containing mailto:info@iost.edu.np in the footer tag and set the keywords "iost", "csit" using Meta tag.
Write an HTML script to insert an audio file in an HTML page using controls and autoplay properties. Also explain how to insert different multimedia elements in HTML.
Explain the concept of semantic web.
Design an HTML form for student registration with the following: name, email, phone, program selection (dropdown), and a submit button.
Write short notes on: (a) Responsive web design (b) Mobile-first approach.

title: JavaScript & Client-Side Programming
sub: JS essentials · DOM · Events · ES6+ · Async · jQuery

h: DOM manipulation and event handling with examples
Arrow functions, spread/rest operator, destructuring (ES6+)
Promises, callbacks, async/await — async flow
Local storage and session storage vs cookies
Event Object in JavaScript
NaN — definition and when it occurs
m: Different ways to include JavaScript in HTML
l: GUI interactions — hover effects, dynamic menus, modals
Write a program displaying background color change every second

qs.t: What is jQuery? What is the use of jQuery? How is jQuery differing from JavaScript?
Explain JavaScript Execution Model.
Explain JavaScript Modules with example.
What is need for client side scripting? Explain features of JavaScript?
What is JavaScript? What are the potential platforms where JavaScript can be used?
What is JavaScript Event? Explain with Example.
What are different data types in JavaScript?
What is Event Object? Explain with Example.
What is NoScript? What are different ways to include JavaScript in an HTML document?
What is NoScript? What is NaN in JavaScript? When does it occur?
What is callback hell?
How do you use local storage in JavaScript?
What are the major differences between Session storage and Cookie storage?
Explain spread operator with example.
List different types of jQuery selectors with one example of each.
What is arrow function? Write rules of writing JSX in React.
Explain Statement, Expression and Keywords in JavaScript.
Write JavaScript code to change background color of web page every second.
Explain different methods that are available to access DOM elements using id, class, name and selector with suitable examples.
Write a program that displays the continuous time in the web page. The time should be in the format HH:MM:SS.
Explain AJAX. List benefits of using AJAX. Demonstrate AJAX with jQuery.

Write a program which includes a function sum(). This function sum() should be designed to add an arbitrary list of parameters. (e.g. if you call the function sum() as sum(1, 2) it should return 3 and if again you call the function sum() as sum(1, 3, 4) it should return 8).
How can you create a simple modal popup using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript that opens and closes on button clicks?
Explain DOM manipulation and event handling using JavaScript with suitable code examples. Demonstrate add/remove DOM nodes dynamically.
List and explain any three features of Modern JavaScript with code snippets (ES6+).
Write short notes on: (a) DevOps principles (b) DevOps lifecycle.

title: React JS & SPA
sub: Components · Props · State · React Router · Fetch API

h: React component-based UI and props — demonstration
State management and data flow (useState)
React JSX rules
Traditional Multi-Page Apps vs SPAs
Principles of React
Fetch API — fetching data, handling errors & loading states
m: Comparison: React vs Angular vs Vue
What is React? Why use it?

qs.t: Explain principles of React.
What are components in React? Demonstrate how props are passed to React components.
What is arrow function? Write rules of writing JSX in React.
Explain Promise with example.

A college wants to develop a Single Page Application (SPA) for course selection using React JS. (A) Draw an architecture diagram for SPA using React (components, props, state, router). (B) Explain the flow of data from a backend API to UI components using fetch() and state hooks. (C) Compare React with Angular and Vue.

title: Server-Side Programming — Django
sub: MVC/MTV · Views · Templates · ORM · Sessions · Middleware

h: MVC Architecture with neat diagram
MTV Architecture with neat diagram
Django file structure — project and app
Django Template Language syntax
What is ORM? Benefits of ORM
Middleware — custom middleware for logging
Form data handling, CSRF, session usage
m: How to set, get and delete sessions
How to set, get and delete cookies from server side
Template inheritance and partial template snippets
l: Comparison: Django vs Flask vs FastAPI vs Spring Boot vs Node.js
What is Templating? Server-side dynamic HTML generation

qs.t: What is middleware? Create custom middleware for logging.
Explain with example syntax of Django Template Language.
What is Templating? How do you generate dynamic HTML content in server side?

What is API versioning and why is it needed? List the three common approaches for API versioning. Explain API structure best practices including consistent response format with JSON examples for success and error responses, and pagination implementation.
What is Django? Explain file structure of Django Project and Django App.
Explain MVC Architecture with neat diagram.
Explain how to set, get and delete sessions.
What is a database? What are types of databases?
How do you inherit templates and how do you include partial template snippets?
What is Database Migration? What are the major differences between Sessions and Cookies?
What are different field types for defining models in Django? Give example.
What are static files? What is ORM? What are benefits of ORM?
Explain MTV Architecture with neat diagram.
Explain how to set, get and delete cookies from server side.
What is Routing? How do you handle routing in server side? Give examples.
Write a Django flow for: (A) URL routing (B) View handling form data (C) Template rendering (D) Session storage. Design the complete request to response cycle using a block diagram.
Explain Model-View-Controller (MVC) architecture with a labeled diagram.
Compare Django, Flask, FastAPI, ASP.NET, Spring Boot, and Node.js.

What are environment variables? Explain why sensitive information like database passwords and API keys should be stored in environment variables instead of source code. Demonstrate how to use environment variables in Django settings.

title: Web Services & APIs
sub: REST · JSON · XML · Serialization · Microservices · CORS

h: REST principles and the six architectural constraints
RESTful API design — HTTP verbs, endpoints, URL design
JSON vs XML — syntax, differences, parsing
Statelessness in REST architecture
What is CORS? Why is it needed in full-stack apps?
Microservices architecture — API gateway, service communication
m: Difference between PUT and PATCH HTTP methods
HTTP status codes — success, client error, server error
What is API (definition), Open APIs vs Internal APIs
Serialization and deserialization in Django REST Framework
l: Building and testing a simple REST API with Postman
Define XML and its syntax rules, structure components

qs.t: How do you parse JSON data in Python using the `json` module? How do you convert Python dictionary back to JSON string? Write a Python program to parse a JSON string containing book information (id, title, author, year, genres array), access and print each field, modify the data (add rating, append to genres), and convert back to JSON.

Define JSON (JavaScript Object Notation). List the syntax rules of JSON. Explain JSON data types with examples and provide a complete JSON example showing simple values, nested objects, arrays, and null values.
Define API (Application Programming Interface). Differentiate between Open APIs and Internal APIs. Explain why APIs are important in modern software development with purposes.
What is REST? Explain the six architectural constraints of REST with their benefits.

What is the difference between PUT and PATCH HTTP methods? What status code is returned when a resource is successfully created using POST? Explain different HTTP status codes with their meanings including from success, from client error, and from server error categories.
What is statelessness in REST architecture? Explain the practices for Endpoints and URL design in RESTful APIs with good and bad examples for each.

Define XML (eXtensible Markup Language) and write syntax rules for XML. Explain XML structure components (Declaration, Element, Attribute, Comment, CDATA) with examples and provide a complete XML example showing nested elements, attributes, and arrays representation.

Define serialization in the context of Django REST Framework. Define deserialization and explain why it is important. Explain the complete serialization and deserialization flow in Django REST Framework for both incoming requests and outgoing responses with step-by-step process.

Define microservices architecture. Define monolithic architecture. Compare monolithic and microservices architecture including key differences (deployment, scaling, database, technology), when to use each approach, and diagram representation of each.
What is CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing)? Differentiate between simple requests and preflight requests. Explain four CORS response headers and their purposes.

Consider a hospital management system designing Microservices Architecture. Explain with an architectural diagram highlighting: (A) API gateway (B) Service communication (C) Independent deployments (D) Database per service strategy.
Explain JSON versus XML, with examples, advantages, and data parsing workflow.
Explain why CORS is needed in full-stack applications.

title: Web Application Security
sub: XSS · SQL Injection · CSRF · Input validation · Auth · Hashing

h: XSS — Stored vs Reflected, four impacts on web apps
SQL Injection — how attacks work, four preventive measures
CSRF — how it exploits sessions, Django CSRF protection with code
Differentiate authentication vs authorization
Password hashing — why plain text is dangerous, key properties
Input validation vs input sanitization
m: HTML encoding and its role in preventing XSS
Why server-side validation is mandatory even with client-side
Cookie security attributes — Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite
Session security concerns — hijacking, fixation, prediction
l: Django settings for secure session cookies
JWT flow and secure token handling

qs.t: Differentiate between authentication and authorization. Explain why storing passwords in plain text is dangerous. Describe the concept of password hashing and its key properties.
Define Cross-Site Scripting (XSS). Differentiate between Stored XSS and Reflected XSS. Explain four potential impacts of XSS attacks on web applications.
Define Cross-Site Request Forgery (CSRF). Explain how CSRF attacks exploit user sessions. Describe how Django's CSRF protection mechanism works with a code example.
Define input sanitization. Explain the difference between input validation and input sanitization. Describe HTML encoding and its role in preventing XSS attacks.

What is input validation? Differentiate between whitelist (allowlist) and blacklist (denylist) validation approaches. Explain why server-side validation is mandatory even when client-side validation is implemented.
What is SQL Injection? Explain how SQL Injection attacks work with an example. Describe four preventive measures against SQL Injection in web applications.
Explain the purpose of the following cookie security attributes: Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite. Write the Django settings to configure secure session cookies.
Explain session-based authentication and its working mechanism. Describe three session security concerns (session hijacking, session fixation, and session prediction) and their prevention methods.
Explain SQL Injection, XSS, and CSRF with examples.

title: Forms, Validation & File Handling
sub: Form design · Server-side validation · File upload · ORM CRUD

h: Design HTML form and write server-side code with validation rules
File upload validation — extension and size checks
Write controller for login with database lookup
Form validation using Django with CSRF
m: Create form with registration fields + full validation
File upload — only images allowed, < 2MB limit
Patient/student data form with specific validation rules
l: Storing validated data to database table

qs.t: Design a form and write corresponding server side code to store the user's values after satisfying the following validation rules: Length of Full name up to 40 characters; Email address must be valid email address; Username must start with string and followed by number; Password length must be more than 8 characters.

Create a HTML page containing a division with image and its description in paragraph. Write a JavaScript function to change the value of description in the paragraph during onmouseover and onmouseout on the image.
Write a server side code to upload a file and validate: File extension (only allow image files: jpg, jpeg, png, gif); File size (must be less than 2MB).

Write a controller that accepts username and password as arguments and checks with student table; if credentials match, redirect to dashboard page, otherwise display 'Invalid username/password'. Assume user already exists.

Create a form to input Name, Email, password. All fields are required. Email should be valid. Phone number should be valid (9********* or 01*******). Password must be at least 8 characters long and contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number and one symbol.

Write server side script to create and validate form with the following rule and store given data into 'patients' table with details (name, patient_id, mobile, gender, address, dob, doctor name): Name, Mobile, Doctor Name, Gender, DOB: Required; Mobile: 10 digit start with 98, 97 or 96; DOB: YYYY-MM-DD format.

Design following forms and write corresponding server side code to store the user's values after satisfying following validation rules. Form fields: TU Registration Number, Email Address, Upload your Project File. Validation: Registration number, email and upload file are mandatory; Email address should be proper email format; Upload file format must include pdf, doc, docx, ppt, pptx, jpeg; File size must be less than 5MB.

Write short notes on: (a) Test automation benefits (b) Code review practices.
Write short notes on: (a) CI/CD pipelines (b) GitHub Actions.
